categories.label: File Exposure
categories.description: Exposed files, open directories, document leaks

dorks.title: Open Directory Listing
dorks.description: Finds web servers with directory browsing enabled. Browse full server file structures and often find sensitive documents.

dorks.title: Parent Directory Access
dorks.description: Targets open directories showing parent directory links — strong indicator of unrestricted server file system access.

dorks.title: Directories with Password Files
dorks.description: Open directories that contain files with 'password' in the name. Often yields credential files left on web servers.

dorks.title: Exposed .htpasswd Files
dorks.description: Apache password files accidentally indexed. Contains usernames and hashed passwords for HTTP basic auth.

dorks.title: Exposed SSH Private Keys
dorks.description: SSH private keys left in publicly accessible directories. Complete server compromise is possible with these.

dorks.title: Exposed .env Files
dorks.description: Environment files containing API keys, database credentials, and app secrets for modern web applications.

dorks.query: filetype:pdf "confidential" OR "internal use only" OR "do not distribute"
dorks.title: Confidential PDF Documents
dorks.description: PDF files labeled confidential or internal. Companies often accidentally expose internal reports and data to Google.

dorks.title: Excel Files with Passwords
dorks.description: Spreadsheet files containing password data — often credential sheets, IT inventory files, or key management docs.

dorks.title: Excel Credential Sheets
dorks.description: Spreadsheets containing both username and password columns. IT teams commonly maintain these for server access management.

dorks.title: SQL Database Dumps
dorks.description: SQL dump files containing user tables. Often include usernames, hashed or plaintext passwords, and full database schemas.

dorks.title: Backup Files
dorks.description: Backup copies often contain older config data, credentials, or source code. Developers frequently forget to restrict access.

dorks.title: Password Log Files
dorks.description: Log files containing cleartext usernames and passwords. Generated by misconfigured applications logging auth attempts.

dorks.title: Password Data Files
dorks.description: DAT files named password.dat containing plaintext username and password combinations used by older applications.

dorks.title: MySQL User Table Dumps
dorks.description: Exported MySQL dumps with user tables containing full database records including usernames, emails, and passwords.

dorks.title: HTTP Server Access Logs
dorks.description: Web server access logs containing full request history — basic auth credentials, trusted IPs, paths, and query strings.

dorks.title: Log Files with Credentials
dorks.description: Application log files that have logged authentication data including usernames and passwords in cleartext.

dorks.title: Outlook Address Books
dorks.description: Microsoft Outlook WAB files containing contact details, email addresses, and phone numbers.

dorks.title: Mailbox Files (MBX)
dorks.description: Outlook v1-4 or Eudora mailbox files exposed on the web. Contains email content and sender addresses.

dorks.title: Exported Email Lists (CSV)
dorks.description: Email contact lists exported in CSV format from CRM systems — often contain names, phones, and full contact details.

dorks.title: Text Files with Email Lists
dorks.description: Plain text files containing email address lists. Often scraped data or exported contact databases left publicly accessible.

dorks.title: Word Docs with Credentials
dorks.description: Microsoft Word documents containing credential data. Often IT documentation, onboarding guides, or password sheets.

dorks.title: Exposed Resumes and CVs
dorks.description: PDF resumes and CVs indexed by Google containing personal information — addresses, phones, emails, employment history.

dorks.title: Backup Directory Listings
dorks.description: Open directories specifically under /backup paths. Often contain database dumps, config backups, and source code archives.

dorks.title: SQL Files with Password Data
dorks.description: Database SQL files containing password references. Often migration scripts or backups with embedded credentials.

dorks.title: PHP Config Files in Directories
dorks.description: Open directories containing config.php files which typically hold database credentials and application secrets.

dorks.title: Exposed Private Keys (.pem)
dorks.description: Private key files for SSL/TLS encryption. Exposure allows full traffic decryption and server impersonation.

dorks.title: PuTTY Private Key Files
dorks.description: PuTTY SSH private key files (.ppk) left in accessible locations. Used for direct SSH access to servers.

dorks.title: RSA Private Keys
dorks.description: Raw RSA private key files. These are the most sensitive files — complete cryptographic identity of a server or user.

dorks.title: Exposed Git Repositories
dorks.description: Git repository folders accessible on web servers. Full source code, commit history, secrets, and credentials in .git/config.

dorks.title: Exposed SVN Repositories
dorks.description: Subversion repository directories accessible on web servers. Source code and commit history fully downloadable.

dorks.title: WordPress Config Files
dorks.description: WordPress configuration files containing database credentials — host, username, password, and database name.

dorks.title: WordPress Upload Text Files
dorks.description: Text files uploaded to WordPress installations. Often contain credentials, notes, or configuration data accidentally uploaded.

categories.label: Login & Auth
categories.description: Admin panels, login portals, authentication pages

dorks.title: Admin Login Pages
dorks.description: Admin panel login pages. Many use default credentials or are vulnerable to brute force. Combine with site: to target a specific domain.

dorks.title: PHP Login Pages
dorks.description: PHP login forms — often unpatched and vulnerable to SQL injection or weak password authentication.

dorks.title: phpMyAdmin Panels
dorks.description: Database admin panels with public access. Often left with default credentials (root with no password). Direct database access.

dorks.query: inurl:"exchange/logon.asp" OR intitle:"Microsoft Outlook Web Access - Logon"
dorks.title: Outlook Web Access (OWA)
dorks.description: Microsoft Exchange email portals. Can be targeted for password spraying to gain access to internal email.

dorks.title: OWA Public Folders (Anonymous)
dorks.description: Outlook Web Access public folders accessible without login. Allows browsing Exchange address book and public folders.

dorks.title: ZyXEL Router Login
dorks.description: ZyXEL Prestige router authentication exposed to the internet. Default credentials admin/1234 or admin/admin commonly unchanged.

dorks.title: Dell Remote Access Controller (DRAC)
dorks.description: DRAC allows full out-of-band remote administration of Dell servers. Exposure gives OS-level control even if server is offline.

dorks.title: Generic Login Portals
dorks.description: Wide net for login forms using common phrasing. Good starting point when you don't know what platform the target uses.

dorks.title: Windows Registry Credential Files
dorks.description: Exported Windows Registry files containing saved default usernames and passwords left exposed on public shares.

dorks.title: Citrix Metaframe Login
dorks.description: Citrix remote access portals. Insecure configurations allow attackers to access internal applications remotely.

dorks.title: Citrix NFuse Portals
dorks.description: Citrix NFuse remote access login pages. Enterprise remote desktop access — gaining entry means full internal network access.

dorks.title: Tarantella Remote Access
dorks.description: Tarantella secure remote access software login pages. Can be used to access site servers or other internal systems.

dorks.title: Apache Tomcat Manager
dorks.description: Tomcat management consoles exposed on port 8080. Default credentials tomcat/tomcat allow WAR file deployment = RCE.

dorks.title: MikroTik RouterOS Panels
dorks.description: MikroTik router management interfaces. Often accessible with default credentials and provide full network control.

dorks.title: pfSense Firewall Panels
dorks.description: pfSense firewall/router management consoles accidentally exposed. Full network control including firewall rules and VPN configs.

dorks.title: WordPress Admin Panels
dorks.description: WordPress admin dashboards excluding wordpress.com. Entry point for plugin upload attacks, credential brute-forcing.

dorks.title: Joomla Admin Panels
dorks.description: Joomla CMS administration login pages. Vulnerable to brute force and default credential attacks.

dorks.title: Django Admin Panels
dorks.description: Django Python web framework admin interfaces. If default superuser not changed, can lead to full application compromise.

dorks.title: Webmin Server Control Panel
dorks.description: Webmin Unix/Linux administration panel on port 10000. Provides full system administration capabilities.

dorks.title: Webmin on Default Port
dorks.description: Webmin instances specifically on default port 10000. Gives complete control over Linux/Unix server.

dorks.title: cPanel Web Hosting Control
dorks.description: cPanel hosting control panels on default ports 2082/2083. Full web hosting management including file manager and databases.

dorks.title: phpPgAdmin PostgreSQL Panels
dorks.description: PostgreSQL database management panels. Similar to phpMyAdmin but for PostgreSQL — direct database access and query execution.

dorks.title: Registry Files with Usernames
dorks.description: Windows registry exports containing username entries. Cross-reference with other dorks to build a full credential profile.

categories.label: Databases
categories.description: Exposed databases, dumps, and DB management interfaces

dorks.title: PHP DB Connection Files
dorks.description: .INC files with unencrypted database credentials. The mysql_connect function exposes host, username, and password.

dorks.title: MySQL Configuration Files
dorks.description: MySQL config files exposing port numbers, version info, and server paths.

dorks.title: SQL GRANT Statements
dorks.description: SQL scripts using IDENTIFIED BY to set database user permissions. Often contain plaintext or encrypted passwords.

dorks.title: Comersus E-commerce Database
dorks.description: Access database from Comersus e-commerce platform. Contains customer records, order history, and payment data.

dorks.title: Microsoft Access User Databases
dorks.description: Access databases named users.mdb used as password stores. Many completely unprotected and directly downloadable.

dorks.title: Access Lock Files (.ldb)
dorks.description: Lock files sharing same name as their database. Replace .ldb with .mdb in URL to download the parent database.

dorks.title: phpMyAdmin Index
dorks.description: Open directory listing for phpMyAdmin. From here, full database management UI accessible for arbitrary SQL.

dorks.title: Live phpMyAdmin Panels
dorks.description: Active phpMyAdmin installations. Default root with no password is common on many shared hosting environments.

dorks.title: SQL Schema with Password Tables
dorks.description: Database schema files revealing table structure and password storage method — crucial for targeted SQL injection.

dorks.title: Database Dump Files
dorks.description: SQL files with database naming conventions in the URL — often full database backups left on public web servers.

dorks.title: Environment Files with DB Password
dorks.description: .env environment files exposing DB_PASSWORD — the standard Laravel/Node format for storing database credentials.

dorks.title: MongoDB Connection Strings
dorks.description: MongoDB connection strings containing credentials. Modern NoSQL databases frequently misconfigured with public access.

dorks.title: Redis Connection Strings
dorks.description: Redis database connection strings often containing auth tokens — Redis is commonly exposed without authentication.

dorks.title: SQL User Data Dumps with Emails
dorks.description: SQL dumps specifically containing email and password data — directly usable for credential stuffing attacks.

categories.label: Config Files
categories.description: Server configs, app configs, and credential-bearing config files

dorks.title: PHP Configuration (php.ini)
dorks.description: PHP's main config file. Contains default database credentials, server paths, error settings, and initialization variables.

dorks.title: Firewall Configuration Files
dorks.description: Firewall configs reveal allowed/denied IP rules, ports, and network topology — key for finding open paths through the firewall.

dorks.title: ProFTPD FTP Configuration
dorks.description: FTP server configuration exposing installation paths, log locations, generic usernames, and group names.

dorks.title: OpenLDAP Configuration
dorks.description: OpenLDAP config including root password in cleartext, plus paths and permissions for all related applications.

dorks.title: LDAP Root Password
dorks.description: slapd.conf files containing the rootpw field — root password for the entire LDAP directory in cleartext or hashed form.

dorks.title: WS_FTP Credentials
dorks.description: WS_FTP config files using extremely weak encryption for stored passwords. Easily crackable with public tools.

dorks.title: Linux Kickstart Root Passwords
dorks.description: Anaconda Linux installer config files containing root passwords in cleartext or MD5 hash for automated provisioning.

dorks.title: GNU Zebra Routing Config
dorks.description: GNU Zebra manages TCP/IP routing protocols. Config files contain enable passwords and full routing topology.

dorks.title: FTP Logs with Auth Paths
dorks.description: WS_FTP log files revealing server paths with htpasswd references — leads to discovering authentication credential file locations.

dorks.title: UnrealIRCd Config Files
dorks.description: IRC server configuration files. May contain oper password and reveal network structure including linked servers.

dorks.title: WordPress DB Config
dorks.description: WordPress wp-config.php files containing database connection credentials — host, username, password, database name.

dorks.title: Laravel .env Database Files
dorks.description: Laravel and other PHP framework .env files exposing database passwords and application secrets.

dorks.title: Django Secret Keys
dorks.description: Django settings files exposing SECRET_KEY — used for session signing. Compromise allows forging session cookies.

dorks.title: AWS Access Keys in Config Files
dorks.description: Amazon Web Services access credentials in configuration files. Full cloud account access — potentially catastrophic.

dorks.title: Private Key Files
dorks.description: Any private key file — SSL/TLS, SSH, or code signing. Fundamental compromise of identity and encryption.

dorks.title: MRTG SNMP Configuration
dorks.description: MRTG configs for polling SNMP devices contain community strings (often 'public') — enables direct SNMP device queries.

dorks.title: YAML Config Files with Passwords
dorks.description: YAML configuration files used by modern apps containing password fields — common in Ruby, Python, and Node.js apps.

dorks.title: Python Settings with Passwords
dorks.description: Python application settings files containing password configuration — often Django or Flask database configurations.

dorks.title: Java Application Properties
dorks.description: Spring Boot and Java application properties files containing database passwords, API keys, and service credentials.

categories.label: Cameras & IoT
categories.description: IP cameras, surveillance systems, and IoT devices

dorks.title: Panasonic Network Cameras
dorks.description: Panasonic IP cameras accessible from a browser. Admin page at /config.html often has no credentials or factory defaults.

dorks.title: Axis Network Cameras
dorks.description: AXIS cameras with embedded web server. Admin button on indexFrame page often works with default credentials.

dorks.title: Mobotix Network Cameras
dorks.description: Mobotix cameras using thttpd web server. High image quality feeds accessible via browser with Open Menu exposed.

dorks.title: Panasonic WJ-NT104 Cameras
dorks.description: Panasonic WJ-NT104 multi-camera monitoring system. Browser-accessible with default credentials frequently unchanged.

dorks.title: Sony SNC-RZ20 Cameras
dorks.description: Sony SNC-RZ20 network cameras. Admin panel accessible at /home/l4/admin.html with default credentials.

dorks.title: Generic IP Camera Viewer
dorks.description: Common URL pattern for web-accessible IP camera viewer interfaces. Works across multiple brands and models.

dorks.title: Axis Live Camera Feeds
dorks.description: Direct live video feed pages from Axis cameras. No authentication required on many installations.

dorks.title: Network Camera Index Pages
dorks.description: Camera system index pages for multiple brands. Entry point to live camera access and control interfaces.

dorks.title: Multi-Camera Frame Systems
dorks.description: Multi-camera surveillance systems with web-based viewing. Often shows multiple camera feeds simultaneously.

dorks.title: Toshiba Network Cameras
dorks.description: Toshiba IP camera login pages. Default credentials (root/ikwb or admin/admin) commonly left unchanged.

dorks.title: Generic Network Camera Homes
dorks.description: Network camera home pages from various manufacturers. Good for broad surveillance system discovery.

dorks.title: D-Link Camera Control Pages
dorks.description: D-Link IP camera user image control pages. Often accessible without authentication on default configurations.

dorks.title: AXIS Live View Application
dorks.description: AXIS camera live view application pages. Real-time video feed accessible from standard web browser.

dorks.title: i-Catcher Surveillance Console
dorks.description: i-Catcher CCTV software web monitoring consoles. Live video feeds from connected cameras accessible publicly.

dorks.title: Axis MJPEG Stream URLs
dorks.description: Direct MJPEG video stream URLs from Axis cameras. Can be opened in VLC or browser for live feed without authentication.

dorks.title: Network Camera Status Pages
dorks.description: IP camera status and time display pages indicating web-accessible camera systems. Entry point to camera control.

dorks.title: Hikvision Camera Portals
dorks.description: Hikvision IP camera web interface entry points. One of the most deployed camera brands globally — often default credentials.

dorks.title: Hikvision Login Pages
dorks.description: Hikvision DVR/NVR and IP camera login pages. Default admin/12345 credentials left unchanged on millions of devices.

dorks.title: Dahua Camera Portals
dorks.description: Dahua IP cameras and DVR systems. Second most deployed surveillance brand globally — widely vulnerable to default creds.

categories.label: Network & Infra
categories.description: Routers, firewalls, printers, and network infrastructure

dorks.title: ADSL Modem Config Pages
dorks.description: Solwise ADSL modem status exposing IP addresses, MAC addresses, subnet masks, and firmware versions.

dorks.title: Belkin Router Status Pages
dorks.description: Belkin Cable/DSL gateway pages with IP addresses, WAN addresses, MAC addresses, firmware, encryption, SSID — a full network profile.

dorks.title: Exposed Network Printers (CUPS)
dorks.description: CUPS print server interfaces exposed to the internet. Attackers can intercept jobs, retrieve stored documents, pivot through network.

dorks.title: Xerox Phaser Printer Config
dorks.description: Xerox Phaser printer configuration panels with completely open access. Can contain POP3 server credentials.

dorks.title: ntop Network Monitor
dorks.description: ntop shows real-time network usage listing all hosts and traffic. Attackers can map internal networks and identify targets.

dorks.title: Network Looking Glass
dorks.description: Looking Glass scripts execute queries on remote routers. Reveals BGP routes, traceroutes, and ASN information.

dorks.title: Visio Network Diagrams
dorks.description: Microsoft Visio network diagrams revealing full topology including internal IPs, firewall locations, and device types.

dorks.title: Lotus Domino Servers
dorks.description: Lists servers running Lotus Domino with descriptive error messages exposing path and OS information.

dorks.title: MikroTik Router Admin
dorks.description: MikroTik RouterOS administration pages. Default credentials admin with no password on many installations.

dorks.title: Network Switch Login Pages
dorks.description: Network switch management login pages exposed to the internet. Gaining access allows full network traffic manipulation.

dorks.title: Cisco Device Show Version Output
dorks.description: Cisco IOS 'show version' command output files revealing exact firmware versions — useful for identifying known CVEs.

dorks.query: intitle:"Cisco Systems" "Please select your login type" -site:cisco.com
dorks.title: Cisco Login Portals
dorks.description: Cisco network device login portals. Entry to routers, switches, and firewalls managing entire network segments.

dorks.title: D-Link HNAP Interfaces
dorks.description: D-Link router HNAP (Home Network Administration Protocol) interfaces. Known for authentication bypass vulnerabilities.

dorks.title: HP Network Printer Status
dorks.description: HP printer network status pages exposing IP config, firmware, and printing history on corporate networks.

dorks.title: Linksys Router Admin
dorks.description: Linksys consumer router administration pages. Default admin credentials commonly not changed on home and small business routers.

dorks.title: NETGEAR Router Panels
dorks.description: NETGEAR router management pages. Default admin/password frequently unchanged — full network control at stake.

categories.label: People & OSINT
categories.description: Find individuals, contacts, profiles, and personal data

dorks.title: Email Lists in Spreadsheets
dorks.description: Spreadsheets containing email addresses — exported contact lists from email clients or CRM systems with names and phones.

dorks.title: LinkedIn Email Profiles
dorks.description: LinkedIn profiles mentioning email addresses publicly. Replace with company name for targeted employee enumeration.

dorks.title: Internal Phone Directories
dorks.description: Private intranet pages accidentally exposed containing employee phone listings and email addresses.

dorks.title: Exposed Company Intranets
dorks.description: Internal company networks with HR sections containing employee directories and org charts.

dorks.title: Unix Email Forwarding Files
dorks.description: Unix .forward files in home directories redirecting email. Reveal email addresses and org email structure.

dorks.title: MSN Messenger Contact Lists
dorks.description: Exported MSN Messenger contact lists. Contains screen names and the user's full contact network.

dorks.title: AIM Buddy Lists
dorks.description: AOL Instant Messenger buddy list files revealing screen names and full contact/blocked lists.

dorks.title: Resumes with Phone Numbers
dorks.description: PDF resumes/CVs containing phone numbers. Cross-reference with LinkedIn for complete personal profiles.

dorks.title: Pastebin Credential Dumps
dorks.description: Pastebin pastes containing Gmail addresses with passwords. Often posted after data breaches.

dorks.title: Pastebin Login Data
dorks.description: Structured credential data on Pastebin — email, username, and password combinations from data breaches.

dorks.title: GitHub Password Files
dorks.description: Password files accidentally committed to public GitHub repositories — extremely common and highly impactful.

dorks.title: GitHub API Key Leaks
dorks.description: API keys and secrets accidentally committed to GitHub. Can include Stripe keys, Twilio, SendGrid, AWS, and more.

dorks.title: CVs with Home Addresses
dorks.description: Curriculum vitae documents containing home addresses. Combine with phone/email dorks for full person profile.

dorks.title: Scribd User Profiles
dorks.description: User profiles on Scribd document sharing. Often contain real names, locations, and uploaded personal documents.

dorks.title: Facebook Phone Profiles
dorks.description: Facebook profiles with phone numbers in the title. Public profiles with personal contact information.

dorks.title: PII Data in Spreadsheets
dorks.description: Spreadsheets containing personally identifiable information including names, dates of birth, and SSNs. Severe privacy violation.

dorks.title: Documents with DOB and Address
dorks.description: PDF documents containing dates of birth and addresses. Could be forms, applications, or records accidentally exposed.

dorks.title: Former Employee LinkedIn Profiles
dorks.description: Ex-employees who may have taken internal knowledge and contacts. Useful for competitive intelligence and social engineering.

dorks.title: Member Directories in Excel
dorks.description: Club, organization, or company member directories exported to Excel. Contains full contact details of all members.

categories.label: Vulnerabilities
categories.description: Error pages, injection points, and vulnerability indicators

dorks.query: intext:"sql syntax near" OR intext:"syntax error" OR intext:"mysql_fetch"
dorks.title: SQL Error Messages
dorks.description: Pages showing SQL errors are strong indicators of SQL injection vulnerabilities — expose DB structure and query params.

dorks.title: ColdFusion SQL Errors
dorks.description: ColdFusion error pages logging SQL statements. Reveal full file paths and exact injection parameters.

dorks.title: PHP File Include Errors
dorks.description: PHP warnings revealing app paths, filename structures, and include paths — identifies local file inclusion vulnerabilities.

dorks.title: ASP.NET Trace Pages
dorks.description: ASP.NET trace pages exposing session IDs, stack traces, port numbers, IP addresses, and full HTTP headers.

dorks.title: PHP File Viewer Scripts
dorks.description: PHP scripts for viewing server files via URL parameters — vulnerable to directory traversal attacks.

dorks.title: ht://Dig Search Errors
dorks.description: ht://Dig errors exposing admin email, cgi-bin path, directory structure, and database locations.

dorks.title: Exposed Windows Recycle Bin
dorks.description: Windows recycle bin exposed on web server. May contain deleted sensitive documents and reveals user SIDs.

dorks.title: Invision Board SQL Errors
dorks.description: SQL error messages from Invision Power Board including connection errors and full PHP file paths.

dorks.title: MySQL Connection Errors
dorks.description: PHP pages showing MySQL connection error messages revealing database hostname, username, and file paths.

dorks.query: intext:"ORA-00933: SQL command not properly ended"
dorks.title: Oracle SQL Errors
dorks.description: Oracle database SQL error messages indicating SQL injection points in enterprise applications.

dorks.title: JavaScript Error Files
dorks.description: JavaScript files showing unhandled exceptions — reveal application logic, API endpoints, and internal function names.

dorks.title: PHP Info Pages
dorks.description: phpinfo() output pages exposing PHP version, loaded modules, server paths, environment variables, and config values.

dorks.title: Apache Server Status Pages
dorks.description: Apache mod_status pages exposing active requests, current connections, server load, and running processes.

dorks.title: Apache Server Info Pages
dorks.description: Apache server-info pages detailing loaded modules, configuration directives, and compiled settings.

dorks.title: Cron Job Log Files
dorks.description: Cron automation logs revealing scheduled tasks, backup locations, usernames, and trusted network host IPs.

dorks.title: Java Stack Traces
dorks.description: Java application stack traces exposing class names, method names, file paths, and internal application structure.

dorks.title: Debug Endpoints
dorks.description: Application debug pages left active in production. Often expose environment variables, database configs, and request data.

dorks.query: intitle:"Struts Problem Report" intext:"Struts has detected an unhandled exception"
dorks.title: Apache Struts Errors
dorks.description: Apache Struts error pages revealing Java class paths, exception details, and server configuration.

dorks.title: WEB-INF Directory Listings
dorks.description: Java web application WEB-INF directories exposed — contains web.xml with servlet configs, often including credentials.

categories.label: Cloud & Modern
categories.description: Cloud services, APIs, modern frameworks, and SaaS platforms

dorks.title: AWS S3 Password Files
dorks.description: Public Amazon S3 buckets containing text files with passwords. S3 misconfiguration is extremely common.

dorks.title: AWS S3 Backup Buckets
dorks.description: Public S3 buckets with backup files. Companies frequently store database dumps and config backups in public S3.

dorks.title: AWS Access Key ID Leaks
dorks.description: AWS access key IDs (starting with AKIA) exposed in files. Full cloud account access possible with matching secret key.

dorks.title: AWS Keys on GitHub
dorks.description: AWS credentials accidentally committed to public GitHub repos. The most common high-impact cloud security mistake.

dorks.title: Stripe API Key Leaks on GitHub
dorks.description: Live Stripe payment API keys exposed on GitHub. sk_live_ prefix indicates production keys — immediate financial risk.

dorks.title: Twilio Auth Tokens on GitHub
dorks.description: Twilio authentication tokens exposed on GitHub. Allows sending SMS and making calls billed to victim's account.

dorks.title: Firebase API Keys on GitHub
dorks.description: Firebase project API keys and config objects on GitHub. Can expose full database and authentication systems.

dorks.title: API Tokens in JSON Files
dorks.description: JSON files containing API tokens for versioned APIs. Often response files accidentally committed to public repos.

dorks.title: Bearer Tokens in Files
dorks.description: JWT Bearer tokens exposed in files. These authenticate API requests and often have long validity periods.

dorks.title: Trello Boards with Passwords
dorks.description: Public Trello boards containing passwords. Teams frequently share credentials on Trello cards without realizing boards are public.

dorks.title: Trello Boards with API Keys
dorks.description: Public Trello boards containing API keys for various services. Common mistake in development teams using Trello for task notes.

dorks.title: Private Keys on Pastebin
dorks.description: RSA private keys pasted on Pastebin — often SSH keys or SSL private keys left by mistake during debugging.

dorks.title: Exposed .git Config Files
dorks.description: Git configuration files on web servers exposing repository URLs, remote settings, and sometimes credentials.

dorks.title: Spring Boot Actuator Endpoints
dorks.description: Spring Boot management endpoints exposing environment variables, config properties, and active beans with credentials.

dorks.title: Spring Boot Health with Secrets
dorks.description: Spring Boot health check endpoints leaking sensitive configuration including database passwords and API credentials.

dorks.title: GitLab Database Password Files
dorks.description: Public GitLab repositories containing database password configuration files — often overlooked in private→public migrations.

dorks.title: GraphQL Playground Instances
dorks.description: Public GraphQL API playgrounds allowing exploration and execution of queries — often expose entire API schema.

dorks.title: Swagger API Documentation
dorks.description: Swagger/OpenAPI documentation pages exposing full API endpoints, parameters, and often allowing direct API calls.
